events.label: assistant
events.text:
Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`.

**Decision: fail** — replacing parts on a P0420 code alone, without first testing the O2 sensors and inspecting the exhaust system, is a diagnostic sequence violation. P0420 is a symptom code (catalyst efficiency below threshold), not a parts-call code; OEM diagnostic trees and OBD-II standards require confirming which component (catalyst, downstream O2 sensor, or an exhaust leak) is actually at fault via waveform/switching-ratio testing before replacement. Skipping straight from code retrieval to parts swap risks unnecessary cost and an unresolved comeback. Corrective action: halt further replacement, run the upstream/downstream O2 sensor test and exhaust leak inspection, confirm root cause, then replace and verify with a drive cycle.
